title: Dense Malaysian Stracciatella-Cheese Bao
description: Herbal and dense, these bao are perfect for a relaxing brunch with friends. Featuring stracciatella cheese and dairy-free-friendly score-fat.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine rice flour, aquafaba, water, yeast, and sugar. Mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a warm, draft-free area and let it rise for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on the size of bao you prefer.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of sesame oil in the pan and cook the bao for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the bao are cooking, prepare the filling by mixing together stracciatella cheese, score-fat, grated ginger, chopped scallions, salt, and black pepper.
Once the bao are cooked, split them in half and fill them with the cheese mixture.
Serve the dense Malaysian stracciatella-cheese bao hot and enjoy!
Garnish with sesame seeds and additional scallions if desired.
